=============================================================================\n17\t// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n18\t// =============================================================================\n19\t\n20\t// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n21\t// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n22\t// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n23\t//\n24\t// Parameters:\n25\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n26\t//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n27\t//\n28\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n29\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n30\t//\n31\t// Example usage:\n32\t//\n33\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n34\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n35\t//\n36\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n37\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n38\t//\n39\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n40\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n41\t//\n42\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n43\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n44\tfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n50\t\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n51\t\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n52\t\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n61\t\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n64\t\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n65\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\treturn parent\n67\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n69\t\t}\n70\t\n71\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n72\t\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n73\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n74\t\n75\t\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n76\t\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n77\t\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n78\t\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for fieldName: The field name\n92\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n93\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n94\t//\n95\t// Returns a formatted location location)\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\treturn ref\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n116\t// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n117\t//\n118\t// Parameters:\n119\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n120\t//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n128\t//\n129\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n130\t//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\n131\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// =============================================================================\n152\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n153\t// =============================================================================\n154\t\n155\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n156\t// This ensures error strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n174\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n175\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n176\t\n177\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n178\t\n179\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n180\t\tif errorType != \"\" {\n181\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n182\t\t}\n183\t\n184\t\t// Add field name if present\n185\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n186\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n187\t\t}\n188\t\n189\t\t// Add message\n190\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n191\t\n192\t\treturn builder.String()\n193\t}\n194\t\n195\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n196\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n197\t//\n198\t// Parameters:\n199\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n200\t//   - expected: The expected value\n201\t//   - actual: The actual value\n202\t//   - customMessage: Optional 404, \"\")\n209\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n210\t//\n211\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n212\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n213\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n214\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n215\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n216\t\n217\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n218\t\n219\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n220\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n221\t\t}\n222\t\n223\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n224\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n225\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n226\t\t} else {\n227\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n228\t\t}\n229\t\n230\t\treturn builder.String()\n231\t}\n232\t\n233\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n234\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n235\t//\n236\t// Parameters:\n237\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n238\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n239\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n240\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n241\t//\n242\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n243\t//\n244\t// Example usage:\n245\t//\n246\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n247\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n248\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n249\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n250\t\n251\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n252\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range maxValue)\n258\t}\n259\t\n260\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n261\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n262\t//\n263\t// Parameters:\n264\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n265\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n266\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n267\t//\n268\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n269\t//\n270\t// Example usage:\n271\t//\n272\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n273\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n274\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n275\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n276\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n277\t\n278\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n279\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n280\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n281\t\t}\n282\t\n283\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n284\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n285\t}\n286\t\n287\t// =============================================================================\n288\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n289\t// =============================================================================\n290\t\n291\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n292\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n293\t//\n294\t// Parameters:\n295\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n296\t//\n297\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n298\t//\n299\t// Example usage:\n300\t//\n301\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n302\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n303\t//\n304\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n305\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n306\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n307\t\tswitch errorType {\n308\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n309\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n310\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n311\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n312\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n313\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n314\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n315\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n316\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n317\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n318\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n319\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n320\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n321\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n322\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n323\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n324\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n325\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n326\t\tdefault:\n327\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n328\t\t}\n329\t}\n330\t\n331\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n332\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n333\t//\n334\t// Parameters:\n335\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n336\t//\n337\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n338\t//\n339\t// Example usage:\n340\t//\n341\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n342\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n343\t//\n344\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n345\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n346\tfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n347\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n348\t\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n349\t}\n350\t\n351\t// =============================================================================\n352\t// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n353\t// =============================================================================\n354\t\n355\t// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n356\t// This CategoryHTTP:\n373\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n374\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n375\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n376\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n377\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n378\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n379\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n380\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n381\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n382\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n383\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n384\t\tdefault:\n385\t\t\treturn string(category)\n386\t\t}\n387\t}\n388\t\n389\t// =============================================================================\n390\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n391\t// =============================================================================\n392\t\n393\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n394\t// This FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n407\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n408\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n409\t\tswitch severity {\n410\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n411\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n412\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n413\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n414\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n415\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n416\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n417\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n418\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n419\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n420\t\tdefault:\n421\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n422\t\t}\n423\t}\n424\t\n425\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n426\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n427\t//\n428\t// Parameters:\n429\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n430\t//\n431\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n432\t//\n433\t// Example usage:\n434\t//\n435\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n436\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n437\t//\n438\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n439\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n440\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n441\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n442\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n443\t\n444\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n445\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n446\t\t}\n447\t\n448\t\treturn label\n449\t}\n450\t\n451\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n452\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n453\t\tswitch severity {\n454\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n455\t\t\treturn \"!\"\n456\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n457\t\t\treturn \"⚠\"\n458\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n459\t\t\treturn \"■\"\n460\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n461\t\t\treturn \"○\"\n462\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n463\t\t\treturn \"i\"\n464\t\tdefault:\n465\t\t\treturn \"?\"\n466\t\t}\n467\t}\n468\t\n469\t// =============================================================================\n470\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n471\t// =============================================================================\n472\t\n473\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n474\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n475\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n476\t//\n477\t// Parameters:\n478\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n479\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n480\t//\n481\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n482\t//\n483\t// Example usage:\n484\t//\n485\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n486\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n487\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n488\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n489\t//\t}\n490\t//\n491\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n492\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n493\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n494\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n495\t\n496\t\t// Add severity if requested\n497\t\tif includeSeverity {\n498\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n499\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n500\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n501\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n502\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n503\t\t\t} else {\n504\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n505\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n506\t\t\t}\n507\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n508\t\t}\n509\t\n510\t\t// Add error type and field\n511\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n512\t\n513\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n514\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n515\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n516\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n517\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n518\t\t\t}\n519\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n520\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n521\t\t\t}\n522\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n523\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n524\t\t\t}\n525\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n526\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\t// Add location if present\n529\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n530\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n531\t\t}\n532\t\n533\t\treturn builder.String()\n534\t}\n535\t\n536\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n537\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n538\t//\n539\t// Parameters:\n540\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n541\t//\n542\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n543\t//\n544\t// Example usage:\n545\t//\n546\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n547\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n548\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n549\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n550\t//\t}\n551\t//\n552\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n553\t//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\n554\tfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n555\t\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n556\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n557\t\t}\n558\t\n559\t\treturn err.ErrorType\n560\t}\n561\t\n562\t// =============================================================================\n563\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n564\t// =============================================================================\n565\t\n566\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n567\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n568\t//\n569\t// Parameters:\n570\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n571\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n572\t//\n573\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n574\t//\n575\t// Example usage:\n576\t//\n577\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n578\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n579\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n580\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n581\t//\t}, {\n582\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n583\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n584\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n585\t//\t}}\n586\t//\n587\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n588\t//\t// Returns:\n589\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n590\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n591\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n592\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n593\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n594\t\t}\n595\t\n596\t\tvar lines []string\n597\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n598\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n599\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n600\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n601\t\t}\n602\t\n603\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n604\t}\n605\t\n606\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n607\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n608\t//\n609\t// Parameters:\n610\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n611\t//\n612\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n613\t//\n614\t// Example usage:\n615\t//\n616\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n617\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n618\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n619\t//\t}, {\n620\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n621\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n622\t//\t}}\n623\t//\n624\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n625\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n626\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n627\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n628\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n629\t\t}\n630\t\n631\t\t// Count errors by type\n632\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n633\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n634\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n635\t\t}\n636\t\n637\t\t// Build summary parts\n638\t\tvar parts []string\n639\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n640\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n641\t\t}\n642\t\n643\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n644\t}\n645\t\n646\t// =============================================================================\n647\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n648\t// =============================================================================\n649\t\n650\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n651\t// without the parent parameter.\n652\t//\n653\t// Parameters:\n654\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n655\t//   - location: Additional location context\n656\t//\n657\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n658\t//\n659\t// Example usage:\n660\t//\n661\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n662\t//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\n663\tfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n664\t\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n665\t}\n666\t\n667\t// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n668\t// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n669\t//\n670\t// Parameters:\n671\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n699\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n700\t//\n701\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n702\tfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n703\t\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n704\t}\n705\t\n706\t// =============================================================================\n707\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n708\t// =============================================================================\n709\t\n710\t// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n711\t// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n712\t//\n713\t// Parameters:\n714\t//   - value: The value to quote\n715\t//\n716\t// Returns a quoted value string.\n717\t//\n718\t// Example usage:\n719\t//\n720\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n721\t//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n722\t//\n723\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n724\t//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\n725\tfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n726\t\tif value == \"\" {\n727\t\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n728\t\t}\n729\t\n730\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n731\t}\n732\t\n733\t// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n734\t// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n735\t// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n736\t//\n737\t// Parameters:\n738\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n739\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n740\t//\n741\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n742\t//\n743\t// Example usage:\n744\t//\n745\t//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n746\t//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\n747\tfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n748\t\tif maxLength < 3 {\n749\t\t\tmaxLength = 3\n750\t\t}\n751\t\n752\t\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n753\t\t\treturn value\n754\t\t}\n755\t\n756\t\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n757\t}\n758\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":"call_4d12fa8563304ff3a7ff8036","session_id":"ca6bc11b-0599-47ad-b084-d9738db071b5","uuid":"1401ff3f-5f85-4d6c-b2c7-83b92f876280","timestamp":"2026-07-14T14:57:54.398Z","subagent_type":"Explore","task_description":"Find FormatFieldReference context"}

FormatStatusCodeRangeError creates a validation error for status code range mismatches.\n193\t//\n194\t// Parameters:\n195\t//   - pattern: The range pattern (e.g., \"4xx\", \"5xx\", \"2xx\")\n196\t//   - actual: The actual status code received\n197\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n198\t//\n199\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate range information and Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n276\t// your specific requirements.\n277\t//\n278\t// Parameters:\n279\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n280\t//   - configuration\"),\n293\t//\t)\n294\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n295\t\tvalidationType string,\n296\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n297\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n298\t) ValidationError {\n299\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n300\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n301\t\t\topt(config)\n302\t\t}\n303\t\n304\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n305\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n306\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n307\t\n308\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n309\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n310\t\t}\n311\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n312\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n313\t\t}\n314\t\tif formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n319\t\t}\n320\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n321\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n322\t\t}\n323\t\tif formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n328\t\t}\n329\t\n330\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// FormatOption {\n367\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n368\t\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n369\t\t}\n370\t}\n371\t\n372\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\n373\tfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n374\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n375\t\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n376\t\t}\n377\t}\n378\t\n379\t// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\n380\tfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n381\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n382\t\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n383\t\t}\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\n387\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n388\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n389\t\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n390\t\t}\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\t// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\n394\tfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n395\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n396\t\t\tc.Suggestions = append(c.Suggestions, suggestions...)\n397\t\t}\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\t// =============================================================================\n401\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n402\t// =============================================================================\n403\t\n404\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n405\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n406\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n407\t\tif err != nil {\n408\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n409\t\t}\n410\t\n411\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n412\t\tif err != nil {\n413\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n414\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n415\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n418\t}\n419\t\n420\t// =============================================================================\n421\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n422\t// =============================================================================\n423\t\n424\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n425\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n426\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n427\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n428\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\t// global tracker instance\n432\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n433\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n437\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n438\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n439\t//\n440\t// Parameters:\n441\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n442\t//\n443\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n444\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n445\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n446\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n447\t\n448\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n449\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n453\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n454\t// configuration issues.\n455\t//\n456\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n457\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n458\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n459\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n460\t\n461\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n462\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n463\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n464\t\t}\n465\t\treturn result\n466\t}\n467\t\n468\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n469\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n470\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n471\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n472\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n473\t\n474\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n478\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n479\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n480\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n481\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n482\t\n483\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t// =============================================================================\n487\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n488\t// =============================================================================\n489\t\n490\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n491\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n492\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n493\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n494\t//\n495\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n496\t// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\n497\t// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n498\t//\n499\t// Error Type Validation:\n500\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n501\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n502\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n503\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n504\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n505\t//\n506\t// Parameters:\n507\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n508\t//   - message: The error message content\n509\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n510\t//\n511\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n512\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n513\t//\n514\t// Example usage:\n515\t//\n516\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n517\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n518\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n519\t//\n520\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n521\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n522\t//\n523\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n524\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n525\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n526\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n527\t//\n528\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n529\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n530\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n531\t//\n532\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n533\t//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n534\t//\n535\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n536\t//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\n537\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n538\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n539\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n540\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n541\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n542\t\n543\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n544\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the original wasn't \"unknown\",\n545\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n546\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && errorType != \"unknown\" {\n547\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n548\t\t}\n549\t\n550\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args\n551\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n552\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n553\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n554\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n557\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n558\t\n559\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n560\t\tif message == \"\" {\n561\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n562\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n563\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n564\t\t\t} else {\n565\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n566\t\t\t}\n567\t\t}\n568\t\n569\t\t// Handle empty errorType - use fallback\n570\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n571\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n572\t\t}\n573\t\n574\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the original error type string\n575\t\t// This maintains backward compatibility - we always use the original string\n576\t\t// even if it wasn't a recognized ErrorType enum value\n577\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n581\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n582\t// instead of string-based error types.\n583\t//\n584\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n585\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n586\t//\n587\t// Parameters:\n588\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n589\t//   - message: The error message content\n590\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n591\t//\n592\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n593\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n594\t//\n595\t// Example usage:\n596\t//\n597\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n598\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n599\t//\n600\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n601\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n602\t//\n603\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n604\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n605\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n606\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n607\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n608\t\n609\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n610\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n611\t\n612\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n613\t\tif message == \"\" {\n614\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n615\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n616\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n617\t\t\t} else {\n618\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n619\t\t\t}\n620\t\t}\n621\t\n622\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n623\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n624\t}\n625\t\n626\t// =============================================================================\n627\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n628\t// =============================================================================\n629\t\n630\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n631\ttype QuoteStyle string\n632\t\n633\tconst (\n634\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n635\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n636\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n637\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n638\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n639\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n640\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n641\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n642\t)\n643\t\n644\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n645\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n646\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n647\t\tprefix     string\n648\t}\n649\t\n650\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n651\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n652\t\n653\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n654\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n655\t//\n656\t// Example usage:\n657\t//\n658\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n659\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n660\t//\n661\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n662\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n663\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n664\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n665\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n666\t\t}\n667\t}\n668\t\n669\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n670\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n671\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n672\t//\n673\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n674\t//\n675\t// Example usage:\n676\t//\n677\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n678\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n679\t//\n680\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n681\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n682\t//\n683\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n684\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n685\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n686\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n687\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n688\t\t}\n689\t}\n690\t\n691\t// =============================================================================\n692\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n693\t// =============================================================================\n694\t\n695\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n696\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n697\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n698\t//\n699\t// Parameters:\n700\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n701\t//\n702\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n703\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n704\t//\n705\t// Example usage:\n706\t//\n707\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n708\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n709\t//\n710\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n711\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n712\t//\n713\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n714\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n715\t//\n716\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n717\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n718\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n719\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n720\t\n721\t\t// Handle empty path\n722\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n723\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n724\t\t}\n725\t\n726\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n727\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n728\t}\n729\t\n730\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n731\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n732\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n733\t\tif path == \"\" {\n734\t\t\treturn \"\"\n735\t\t}\n736\t\n737\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n738\t\tvar result []string\n739\t\n740\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n741\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n742\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n743\t\t\t\tcontinue\n744\t\t\t}\n745\t\n746\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n747\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n748\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n749\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n750\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n751\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n752\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n753\t\t\t\t} else {\n754\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n755\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n756\t\t\t\t}\n757\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n758\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n759\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n760\t\t\t} else {\n761\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n762\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n763\t\t\t}\n764\t\t}\n765\t\n766\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n767\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n768\t\t}\n769\t\n770\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n771\t}\n772\t\n773\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n774\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n775\t\tif s == \"\" {\n776\t\t\treturn false\n777\t\t}\n778\t\n779\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n780\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n781\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n782\t\t\t\treturn false\n783\t\t\t}\n784\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n785\t\t}\n786\t\n787\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n788\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n789\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n790\t\t\t\treturn false\n791\t\t\t}\n792\t\t}\n793\t\n794\t\treturn true\n795\t}\n796\t\n797\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n798\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n799\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n800\t}\n801\t\n802\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n803\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n804\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n805\t// for field names through variadic options.\n806\t//\n807\t// Parameters:\n808\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n809\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n810\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n811\t//\n812\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n813\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n814\t//\n815\t// Example usage:\n816\t//\n817\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n818\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n819\t//\n820\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n821\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n822\t//\n823\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n824\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n825\t//\n826\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n827\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n828\t//\n829\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n830\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n831\t//\n832\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n833\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n834\t//\n835\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n836\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n837\t//\n838\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n839\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n840\t//\n841\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n842\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n843\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n844\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n845\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n846\t\n847\t\t// Apply options\n848\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n849\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n850\t\t\topt(config)\n851\t\t}\n852\t\n853\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n854\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n855\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n856\t\t}\n857\t\n858\t\t// Handle empty field path\n859\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n860\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n861\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n862\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n863\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n864\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n865\t\t\t\t}\n866\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n867\t\t\t}\n868\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n869\t\t}\n870\t\n871\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n872\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n873\t\n874\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n875\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n876\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n877\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n878\t\t\t}\n879\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n880\t\t}\n881\t\n882\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n883\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n884\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n885\t\t}\n886\t\n887\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n888\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n889\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n890\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n891\t\n892\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n893\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n894\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n895\t\t\t}\n896\t\n897\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n898\t\t}\n899\t\n900\t\treturn normalizedPath\n901\t}\n902\t\n903\t// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n904\t// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n905\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n906\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n907\t\t\treturn path\n908\t\t}\n909\t\n910\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n911\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n912\t\tinBrackets := false\n913\t\n914\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n915\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n916\t\n917\t\t\tswitch {\n918\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n919\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n920\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n921\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n922\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n923\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n924\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n925\t\t\t\t}\n926\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n927\t\n928\t\t\tcase r == '[':\n929\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n930\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n931\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n932\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n933\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n934\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n935\t\t\t\t}\n936\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n937\t\t\t\tinBrackets TestGetInvalidErrorTypes verifies that GetInvalidErrorTypes returns\n1433\t// a copy of the tracking map to prevent concurrent modification.\n1434\tfunc TestGetInvalidErrorTypes(t *testing.T) {\n1435\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1436\t\n1437\t\t// Add some tracked types\n1438\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type1\")\n1439\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(\"type2\")\n1440\t\n1441\t\t// Get the again\n1449\t\ttrackedAgain := GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n1450\t\n1451\t\t// Verify that modifications to the returned map don't affect the internal state\n1452\t\tif trackedAgain[\"type1\"] == 999 {\n1453\t\t\tt.Error(\"Modifying the returned map should not affect internal tracking state\")\n1454\t\t}\n1455\t\tif _, ok := trackedAgain[\"new_type\"]; ok {\n1456\t\t\tt.Error(\"Adding to the returned map should not affect internal tracking state\")\n1457\t\t}\n1458\t\n1459\t\t// Verify actual counts\n1460\t\tif count := trackedAgain[\"type1\"]; count != 1 {\n1461\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected type1 count to be 1, got %d\", count)\n1462\t\t}\n1463\t}\n1464\t\n1465\t// TestResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking verifies that reset clears all tracked types.\n1466\tfunc {\n1493\t\tResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n1494\t\n1495\t\t// Initially should be 0\n1496\t\tif InvalidErrorTypeCount() != 0 {\n1497\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"Expected initial count to be 0, got %d\", inputs.\n1889\tfunc TestFormatFieldPath_Consistency(t *testing.T) {\n1890\t\t// Different ways to represent the same field path should normalize to the same output\n1891\t\tequivalentPaths := [][]string{\n1892\t\t\t// Array access with dot notation vs bracket notation\n1893\t\t\t{\"users.0.email\", \"users[0].email\"},\n1894\t\t\t// Multiple array indices\n1895\t\t\t{\"data.0.items.1.name\", \"data[0].items[1].name\"},\n1896\t\t\t// Mixed notation\n1897\t\t\t{\"users[0].emails.1.address\", \"users.0.emails.1.address\"},\n1898\t\t}\n1899\t\n1900\t\tfor _, paths := range equivalentPaths {\n1901\t\t\tif len(paths) < 2 {\n1902\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1903\t\t\t}\n1904\t\n1905\t\t\tfirstResult := FormatFieldPath(paths[0])\n1906\t\t\tfor _, path := range paths[1:] {\n1907\t\t\t\tresult := FormatFieldPath(path)\n1908\t\t\t\tif result != firstResult {\n1909\t\t\t\t\tt.Errorf(\"FormatFieldPath(%q) = %q, but FormatFieldPath(%q) = %q (expected same result)\",\n1910\t\t\t\t\t\tpaths[0], firstResult, path, result)\n1911\t\t\t\t}\n1912\t\t\t}\n1913\t\t}\n1914\t}\n1915\t\n1916\t// \\\"application/json\\\")\\n247\\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\\n248\\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\\n249\\t//\\n250\\t// Returns a ValidationError with needed\\n263\\t//\\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\\n264\\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\\n265\\t\\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\\\"content_type\\\").\\n266\\t\\t\\tWithExpected(expected).\\n267\\t\\t\\tWithActual(actual).\\n268\\t\\t\\tWithContext(context).\\n269\\t\\t\\tFormat()\\n270\\t}\\n271\\t\\n272\\t// =============================================================================\\n334\\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\\n335\\t// =============================================================================\\n403\\t\\n404\\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\\n405\\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern err\\n409\\t\\t}\\n410\\t\\n411\\t\\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\\n412\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n413\\t\\t\\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\\n414\\t\\t\\tdesc = \\\"\\\"\\n415\\t\\t}\\n416\\t\\n417\\t\\treturn min, max, desc, nil\\n418\\t}\\n419\\t\\n420\\t// =============================================================================\\n421\\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\\n422\\t// =============================================================================\\n423\\t\\n424\\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\\n425\\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\\n426\\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\\n427\\t\\tmu       sync.RWMutex\\n428\\t\\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\\n429\\t}\\n430\\t\\n431\\t// global tracker instance\\n432\\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\\n433\\t\\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\\n434\\t}\\n435\\t\\n436\\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\\n437\\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\\n438\\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\\n439\\t//\\n440\\t// Parameters:\\n441\\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\\n442\\t//\\n443\\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\\n444\\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\\n445\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\\n446\\t\\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\\n447\\t\\n448\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\\n449\\t\\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\\n450\\t}\\n451\\t\\n452\\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\\n453\\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\\n454\\t// configuration issues.\\n455\\t//\\n456\\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\\n457\\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\\n458\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\\n459\\t\\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\\n460\\t\\n461\\t\\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\\n462\\t\\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\\n463\\t\\t\\tresult[k] = v\\n464\\t\\t}\\n465\\t\\treturn result\\n466\\t}\\n467\\t\\n468\\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\\n469\\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\\n470\\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\\n471\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\\n472\\t\\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\\n473\\t\\n474\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\\n475\\t}\\n476\\t\\n477\\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\\n478\\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\\n479\\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\\n480\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\\n481\\t\\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\\n482\\t\\n483\\t\\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\\n484\\t}\\n485\\t\\n486\\t// =============================================================================\\n487\\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\\n488\\t// =============================================================================\\n489\\t\\n490\\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\\n491\\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\\n492\\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\\n493\\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\\n494\\t//\\n495\\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\\n496\\t// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\\n497\\t// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\\n498\\t//\\n499\\t// Error Type Validation:\\n500\\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \\\"required\\\", \\\"format\\\", \\\"range\\\")\\n501\\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\\n502\\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\\n503\\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\\n504\\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\\n505\\t//\\n506\\t// Parameters:\\n507\\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \\\"required\\\", \\\"format\\\", \\\"status_code\\\")\\n508\\t//   - message: The error message content\\n509\\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\\n510\\t//\\n511\\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\\n512\\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\\n513\\t//\\n514\\t// Example usage:\\n515\\t//\\n516\\t//\\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\\n517\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"required\\\", \\\"This field is required\\\", \\\"email\\\")\\n518\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[required] email: This field is required\\\"\\n519\\t//\\n520\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"format\\\", \\\"Invalid email format\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n521\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[format] Invalid email format\\\"\\n522\\t//\\n523\\t//\\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\\n524\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"custom_validation\\\", \\\"Custom check failed\\\", \\\"field\\\")\\n525\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\\\"\\n526\\t//\\t// \\\"custom_validation\\\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\\n527\\t//\\n528\\t//\\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\\n529\\t//\\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\\n530\\t//\\t// Returns: map[string]int{\\\"custom_validation\\\": 1, ...}\\n531\\t//\\n532\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"\\\", \\\"Something went wrong\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n533\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[error] Something went wrong\\\"\\n534\\t//\\n535\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"validation\\\", \\\"\\\", \\\"email\\\")\\n536\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[validation] email: email validation failed\\\"\\n537\\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\\n538\\t\\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\\n539\\t\\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\\n540\\t\\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\\n541\\t\\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\\n542\\t\\n543\\t\\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\\n544\\t\\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the original wasn't \\\"unknown\\\",\\n545\\t\\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\\n546\\t\\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \\\"\\\" && errorType != \\\"unknown\\\" {\\n547\\t\\t\\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\\n548\\t\\t}\\n549\\t\\n550\\t\\t// Extract field name from variadic args\\n551\\t\\tfieldNameStr := \\\"\\\"\\n552\\t\\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\\n553\\t\\t\\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\\n554\\t\\t}\\n555\\t\\n556\\t\\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\\n557\\t\\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\\n558\\t\\n559\\t\\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\\n560\\t\\tif message == \\\"\\\" {\\n561\\t\\t\\t// Check if field name was provided\\n562\\t\\t\\tif fieldNameStr != \\\"\\\" {\\n563\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s validation failed\\\", fieldNameStr)\\n564\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n565\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = \\\"(no message provided)\\\"\\n566\\t\\t\\t}\\n567\\t\\t}\\n568\\t\\n569\\t\\t// Handle empty errorType - use fallback\\n570\\t\\tif errorType == \\\"\\\" {\\n571\\t\\t\\terrorType = \\\"error\\\"\\n572\\t\\t}\\n573\\t\\n574\\t\\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the original error type string\\n575\\t\\t// This maintains backward compatibility - we always use the original string\\n576\\t\\t// even if it wasn't a recognized ErrorType enum value\\n577\\t\\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\\n578\\t}\\n579\\t\\n580\\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\\n581\\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\\n582\\t// instead of string-based error types.\\n583\\t//\\n584\\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\\n585\\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\\n586\\t//\\n587\\t// Parameters:\\n588\\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\\n589\\t//   - message: The error message content\\n590\\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\\n591\\t//\\n592\\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\\n593\\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\\n594\\t//\\n595\\t// Example usage:\\n596\\t//\\n597\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \\\"Field is required\\\", \\\"email\\\")\\n598\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[required] email: Field is format\\\"\\n602\\t//\\n603\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \\\"Value out of range\\\", \\\"age\\\")\\n604\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[range] age: Value out of range\\\"\\n605\\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\\n606\\t\\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\\n607\\t\\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\\n608\\t\\n609\\t\\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\\n610\\t\\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\\n611\\t\\n612\\t\\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\\n613\\t\\tif message == \\\"\\\" {\\n614\\t\\t\\t// Check if field name was provided\\n615\\t\\t\\tif fieldName != \\\"\\\" {\\n616\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s validation failed\\\", fieldName)\\n617\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n618\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = \\\"(no message provided)\\\"\\n619\\t\\t\\t}\\n620\\t\\t}\\n621\\t\\n622\\t\\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\\n623\\t\\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\\n624\\t}\\n625\\t\\n626\\t// =============================================================================\\n627\\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\\n628\\t// =============================================================================\\n629\\t\\n630\\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\\n631\\ttype QuoteStyle string\\n632\\t\\n633\\tconst (\\n634\\t\\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\\n635\\t\\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \\\"\\\"\\n636\\t\\t// SingleQuote applies \\\"\\\\\\\"\\\"\\n640\\t\\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\\n641\\t\\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \\\"`\\\"\\n642\\t)\\n643\\t\\n644\\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\\n645\\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\\n646\\t\\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\\n647\\t\\tprefix     string\\n648\\t}\\n649\\t\\n650\\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\\n651\\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\\n652\\t\\n653\\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\\n654\\t// The =============================================================================\\n694\\t\\n695\\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\\n696\\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\\n697\\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\\n698\\t//\\n699\\t// Parameters:\\n700\\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \\\"user.email\\\", \\\"users.0.email\\\", \\\"data.items[5]\\\")\\n701\\t//\\n702\\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\\n703\\t// Empty or invalid paths return \\\"(unknown field)\\\" for consistent error display.\\n704\\t//\\n705\\t// Example usage:\\n706\\t//\\n707\\t//\\tref := \\\"users[0].email\\\"\\n712\\t//\\n713\\t//\\tref := FormatFieldPath(\\\"data.items[5]\\\")\\n714\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"data.items[5]\\\"\\n715\\t//\\n716\\t//\\tref := FormatFieldPath(\\\"\\\")\\n717\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"(unknown field)\\\"\\n718\\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\\n719\\t\\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\\n720\\t\\n721\\t\\t// Handle empty path\\n722\\t\\tif fieldPath == \\\"\\\" {\\n723\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"(unknown field)\\\"\\n724\\t\\t}\\n725\\t\\n726\\t\\t// Parse and normalize the field path\\n727\\t\\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\\n728\\t}\\n729\\t\\n730\\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\\n731\\t// It converts \\\"users.0.email\\\" to \\\"users[0].email\\\" format for consistency.\\n732\\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\\n733\\t\\tif path == \\\"\\\" {\\n734\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n735\\t\\t}\\n736\\t\\n737\\t\\tparts := strings.Split(path, \\\".\\\")\\n738\\t\\tvar result []string\\n739\\t\\n740\\t\\tfor _, part := range parts {\\n741\\t\\t\\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\\n742\\t\\t\\tif part == \\\"\\\" {\\n743\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n744\\t\\t\\t}\\n745\\t\\n746\\t\\t\\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\\n747\\t\\t\\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\\n748\\t\\t\\t\\t// Convert \\\".0\\\" to \\\"[0]\\\" format\\n749\\t\\t\\t\\tif len(result) > 0 {\\n750\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Append to previous part as array index\\n751\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\\n752\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s[%s]\\\", result[lastIdx], part)\\n753\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n754\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\\n755\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, part)\\n756\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n757\\t\\t\\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\\n758\\t\\t\\t\\t// Already has array notation like \\\"items[5]\\\" - keep as is\\n759\\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, part)\\n760\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n761\\t\\t\\t\\t// Regular field name\\n762\\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, part)\\n763\\t\\t\\t}\\n764\\t\\t}\\n765\\t\\n766\\t\\tif len(result) == 0 {\\n767\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"(unknown field)\\\"\\n768\\t\\t}\\n769\\t\\n770\\t\\treturn strings.Join(result, \\\".\\\")\\n771\\t}\\n772\\t\\n773\\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\\n774\\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\\n775\\t\\tif s == \\\"\\\" {\\n776\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n777\\t\\t}\\n778\\t\\n779\\t\\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\\n780\\t\\tif s[0] == '-' {\\n781\\t\\t\\tif len(s) == 1 {\\n782\\t\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"application/json\\\")\\n247\\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\\n248\\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\\n249\\t//\\n250\\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\\n251\\t//\\n252\\t// Example usage:\\n253\\t//\\n254\\t//\\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\\\"application/json\\\", \\\"text/html\\\", \\\"API response\\\")\\n255\\t//\\t// Output:\\n256\\t//\\t// content_type validation failed\\n257\\t//\\t//   Expected: application/json\\n258\\t//\\t//   Actual:   text/html\\n259\\t//   Context:  API response\\n260\\t//\\t//   Suggestions:\\n261\\t//\\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\\n262\\t//\\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\\n263\\t//\\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\\n264\\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\\n265\\t\\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\\\"content_type\\\").\\n266\\t\\t\\tWithExpected(expected).\\n267\\t\\t\\tWithActual(actual).\\n268\\t\\t\\tWithContext(context).\\n269\\t\\t\\tFormat()\\n270\\t}\\n271\\t\\n272\\t// custom suggestions. \\\"actual_value\\\"}`),\\n292\\t//\\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\\\"Check field value\\\", \\\"Verify configuration\\\"),\\n293\\t//\\t)\\n294\\tfunc &FormatConfig{}\\n300\\t\\tfor _, opt := range options {\\n301\\t\\t\\topt(config)\\n302\\t\\t}\\n303\\t\\n304\\t\\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\\n305\\t\\t\\tWithExpected(expected).\\n306\\t\\t\\tWithActual(actual)\\n307\\t\\n308\\t\\tif config.Context != \\\"\\\" {\\n309\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\\n310\\t\\t}\\n311\\t\\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \\\"\\\" {\\n312\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\\n313\\t\\t}\\n314\\t\\tif config.FieldName != \\\"\\\" {\\n315\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\\n316\\t\\t}\\n317\\t\\tif config.PatternDetails != \\\"\\\" {\\n318\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\\n319\\t\\t}\\n320\\t\\tif config.RangeInfo != \\\"\\\" {\\n321\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\\n322\\t\\t}\\n323\\t\\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\\n324\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\\n325\\t\\t}\\n326\\t\\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\\n327\\t\\t\\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\\n328\\t\\t}\\n329\\t\\n330\\t\\treturn formatter.Format()\\n331\\t}\\n332\\t\\n333\\t// =============================================================================\\n334\\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\\n335\\t// =============================================================================\\n336\\t\\n337\\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\\n338\\ttype FormatConfig
