=============================================================================\\n403\\t\\n404\\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\\n405\\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\\n406\\t\\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\\n407\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n408\\t\\t\\treturn 0, 0, \\\"\\\", err\\n409\\t\\t}\\n410\\t\\n411\\t\\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\\n412\\t\\tif err != nil {\\n413\\t\\t\\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\\n414\\t\\t\\tdesc = \\\"\\\"\\n415\\t\\t}\\n416\\t\\n417\\t\\treturn min, max, desc, nil\\n418\\t}\\n419\\t\\n420\\t// =============================================================================\\n421\\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\\n422\\t// =============================================================================\\n423\\t\\n424\\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\\n425\\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\\n426\\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\\n427\\t\\tmu       sync.RWMutex\\n428\\t\\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\\n429\\t}\\n430\\t\\n431\\t// global tracker instance\\n432\\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\\n433\\t\\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\\n434\\t}\\n435\\t\\n436\\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\\n437\\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\\n438\\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\\n439\\t//\\n440\\t// Parameters:\\n441\\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\\n442\\t//\\n443\\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\\n444\\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\\n445\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\\n446\\t\\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\\n447\\t\\n448\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\\n449\\t\\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\\n450\\t}\\n451\\t\\n452\\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\\n453\\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\\n454\\t// configuration issues.\\n455\\t//\\n456\\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\\n457\\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\\n458\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\\n459\\t\\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\\n460\\t\\n461\\t\\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\\n462\\t\\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\\n463\\t\\t\\tresult[k] = v\\n464\\t\\t}\\n465\\t\\treturn result\\n466\\t}\\n467\\t\\n468\\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\\n469\\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\\n470\\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\\n471\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\\n472\\t\\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\\n473\\t\\n474\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\\n475\\t}\\n476\\t\\n477\\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\\n478\\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\\n479\\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\\n480\\t\\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\\n481\\t\\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\\n482\\t\\n483\\t\\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\\n484\\t}\\n485\\t\\n486\\t// =============================================================================\\n487\\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\\n488\\t// =============================================================================\\n489\\t\\n490\\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\\n491\\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\\n492\\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\\n493\\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\\n494\\t//\\n495\\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\\n496\\t// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\\n497\\t// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\\n498\\t//\\n499\\t// Error Type Validation:\\n500\\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \\\"required\\\", \\\"format\\\", \\\"range\\\")\\n501\\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\\n502\\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\\n503\\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\\n504\\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\\n505\\t//\\n506\\t// Parameters:\\n507\\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \\\"required\\\", \\\"format\\\", \\\"status_code\\\")\\n508\\t//   - message: The error message content\\n509\\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\\n510\\t//\\n511\\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\\n512\\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\\n513\\t//\\n514\\t// Example usage:\\n515\\t//\\n516\\t//\\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\\n517\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"required\\\", \\\"This field is required\\\", \\\"email\\\")\\n518\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[required] email: This field is required\\\"\\n519\\t//\\n520\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"format\\\", \\\"Invalid email format\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n521\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[format] Invalid email format\\\"\\n522\\t//\\n523\\t//\\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\\n524\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"custom_validation\\\", \\\"Custom check failed\\\", \\\"field\\\")\\n525\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\\\"\\n526\\t//\\t// \\\"custom_validation\\\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\\n527\\t//\\n528\\t//\\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\\n529\\t//\\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\\n530\\t//\\t// Returns: map[string]int{\\\"custom_validation\\\": 1, ...}\\n531\\t//\\n532\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"\\\", \\\"Something went wrong\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n533\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[error] Something went wrong\\\"\\n534\\t//\\n535\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\\\"validation\\\", \\\"\\\", \\\"email\\\")\\n536\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[validation] email: email validation failed\\\"\\n537\\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\\n538\\t\\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\\n539\\t\\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\\n540\\t\\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\\n541\\t\\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\\n542\\t\\n543\\t\\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\\n544\\t\\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the original wasn't \\\"unknown\\\",\\n545\\t\\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\\n546\\t\\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \\\"\\\" && errorType != \\\"unknown\\\" {\\n547\\t\\t\\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\\n548\\t\\t}\\n549\\t\\n550\\t\\t// Extract field name from variadic args\\n551\\t\\tfieldNameStr := \\\"\\\"\\n552\\t\\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\\n553\\t\\t\\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\\n554\\t\\t}\\n555\\t\\n556\\t\\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\\n557\\t\\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\\n558\\t\\n559\\t\\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\\n560\\t\\tif message == \\\"\\\" {\\n561\\t\\t\\t// Check if field name was provided\\n562\\t\\t\\tif fieldNameStr != \\\"\\\" {\\n563\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s validation failed\\\", fieldNameStr)\\n564\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n565\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = \\\"(no message provided)\\\"\\n566\\t\\t\\t}\\n567\\t\\t}\\n568\\t\\n569\\t\\t// Handle empty errorType - use fallback\\n570\\t\\tif errorType == \\\"\\\" {\\n571\\t\\t\\terrorType = \\\"error\\\"\\n572\\t\\t}\\n573\\t\\n574\\t\\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the original error type string\\n575\\t\\t// This maintains backward compatibility - we always use the original string\\n576\\t\\t// even if it wasn't a recognized ErrorType enum value\\n577\\t\\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\\n578\\t}\\n579\\t\\n580\\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\\n581\\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\\n582\\t// instead of string-based error types.\\n583\\t//\\n584\\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\\n585\\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\\n586\\t//\\n587\\t// Parameters:\\n588\\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\\n589\\t//   - message: The error message content\\n590\\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\\n591\\t//\\n592\\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\\n593\\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\\n594\\t//\\n595\\t// Example usage:\\n596\\t//\\n597\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \\\"Field is required\\\", \\\"email\\\")\\n598\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[required] email: Field is required\\\"\\n599\\t//\\n600\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \\\"Invalid email format\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n601\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[format] Invalid email format\\\"\\n602\\t//\\n603\\t//\\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \\\"Value out of range\\\", \\\"age\\\")\\n604\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"[range] age: Value out of range\\\"\\n605\\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\\n606\\t\\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\\n607\\t\\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\\n608\\t\\n609\\t\\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\\n610\\t\\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\\n611\\t\\n612\\t\\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\\n613\\t\\tif message == \\\"\\\" {\\n614\\t\\t\\t// Check if field name was provided\\n615\\t\\t\\tif fieldName != \\\"\\\" {\\n616\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s validation failed\\\", fieldName)\\n617\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n618\\t\\t\\t\\tmessage = \\\"(no message provided)\\\"\\n619\\t\\t\\t}\\n620\\t\\t}\\n621\\t\\n622\\t\\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\\n623\\t\\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\\n624\\t}\\n625\\t\\n626\\t// =============================================================================\\n627\\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\\n628\\t// =============================================================================\\n629\\t\\n630\\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\\n631\\ttype QuoteStyle string\\n632\\t\\n633\\tconst (\\n634\\t\\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\\n635\\t\\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \\\"\\\"\\n636\\t\\t// SingleQuote applies \\\"\\\\\\\"\\\"\\n640\\t\\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\\n641\\t\\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \\\"`\\\"\\n642\\t)\\n643\\t\\n644\\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\\n645\\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\\n646\\t\\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\\n647\\t\\tprefix     string\\n648\\t}\\n649\\t\\n650\\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\\n651\\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\\n652\\t\\n653\\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\\n654\\t// The \\\"response\\\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\\n659\\t//\\t// Returns: response.\\\"user\\\".\\\"email\\\"\\n660\\t//\\n661\\t//\\tref := FormatFieldReference(\\\"users.0.email\\\", \\\"\\\", {\\n665\\t\\t\\tc.quoteStyle = style\\n666\\t\\t}\\n667\\t}\\n668\\t\\n669\\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\\n670\\t// The prefix is added before the =============================================================================\\n694\\t\\n695\\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\\n696\\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\\n697\\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\\n698\\t//\\n699\\t// Parameters:\\n700\\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \\\"user.email\\\", \\\"users.0.email\\\", \\\"data.items[5]\\\")\\n701\\t//\\n702\\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\\n703\\t// Empty or invalid paths return \\\"(unknown field)\\\" for consistent error display.\\n704\\t//\\n705\\t// Example usage:\\n706\\t//\\n707\\t//\\tref := \\\"users[0].email\\\"\\n712\\t//\\n713\\t//\\tref := FormatFieldPath(\\\"data.items[5]\\\")\\n714\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"data.items[5]\\\"\\n715\\t//\\n716\\t//\\tref := FormatFieldPath(\\\"\\\")\\n717\\t//\\t// Returns: \\\"(unknown field)\\\"\\n718\\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\\n719\\t\\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\\n720\\t\\n721\\t\\t// Handle empty path\\n722\\t\\tif fieldPath == \\\"\\\" {\\n723\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"(unknown field)\\\"\\n724\\t\\t}\\n725\\t\\n726\\t\\t// Parse and normalize the field path\\n727\\t\\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\\n728\\t}\\n729\\t\\n730\\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\\n731\\t// It converts \\\"users.0.email\\\" to \\\"users[0].email\\\" format for consistency.\\n732\\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\\n733\\t\\tif path == \\\"\\\" {\\n734\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"\\\"\\n735\\t\\t}\\n736\\t\\n737\\t\\tparts := strings.Split(path, \\\".\\\")\\n738\\t\\tvar result []string\\n739\\t\\n740\\t\\tfor _, part := range parts {\\n741\\t\\t\\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\\n742\\t\\t\\tif part == \\\"\\\" {\\n743\\t\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n744\\t\\t\\t}\\n745\\t\\n746\\t\\t\\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\\n747\\t\\t\\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\\n748\\t\\t\\t\\t// Convert \\\".0\\\" to \\\"[0]\\\" format\\n749\\t\\t\\t\\tif len(result) > 0 {\\n750\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Append to previous part as array index\\n751\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\\n752\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"%s[%s]\\\", result[lastIdx], part)\\n753\\t\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n754\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\\n755\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, part)\\n756\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n757\\t\\t\\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\\n758\\t\\t\\t\\t// Already has array notation like \\\"items[5]\\\" - keep as is\\n759\\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, part)\\n760\\t\\t\\t} else {\\n761\\t\\t\\t\\t// Regular field name\\n762\\t\\t\\t\\tresult = append(result, part)\\n763\\t\\t\\t}\\n764\\t\\t}\\n765\\t\\n766\\t\\tif len(result) == 0 {\\n767\\t\\t\\treturn \\\"(unknown field)\\\"\\n768\\t\\t}\\n769\\t\\n770\\t\\treturn strings.Join(result, \\\".\\\")\\n771\\t}\\n772\\t\\n773\\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\\n774\\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\\n775\\t\\tif s == \\\"\\\" {\\n776\\t\\t\\treturn false\\n777\\t\\t}\\n778\\t\\n779\\t\\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\\n780\\t\\tif s[0] == '-' {\\n781\\t\\t\\tif len(s) == 1 {\\n782\\t\\t\\t\\treturn max))\n227\t\t}\n228\t\n229\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code_range\").\n230\t\t\tWithExpected(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", pattern, desc)).\n231\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n232\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n233\t\t\tWithValidationDetails(details...)\n234\t\n235\t\t// Only add range info if the pattern is valid\n236\t\tif err == nil {\n237\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(rangeInfo)\n238\t\t}\n239\t\n240\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n241\t}\n242\t\n243\t// FormatContentTypeError creates a validation error for Content-Type header mismatches.\n244\t//\n245\t// Parameters:\n246\t//   - expected: The expected content type (e.g., \"application/json\")\n247\t//   - actual: The actual content type received\n248\t//   - context: Optional context about the validation\n249\t//\n250\t// Returns a ValidationError with appropriate suggestions.\n251\t//\n252\t// Example usage:\n253\t//\n254\t//\terr := validate.FormatContentTypeError(\"application/json\", \"text/html\", \"API response\")\n255\t//\t// Output:\n256\t//\t// content_type validation failed\n257\t//\t//   Expected: application/json\n258\t//\t//   Actual:   text/html\n259\t//   Context:  API response\n260\t//\t//   Suggestions:\n261\t//\t//     - Verify Content-Type header matches request body format\n262\t//\t//     - Check if charset or boundary parameters are needed\n263\t//\t//     - Ensure the body is properly formatted for the content type\n264\tfunc FormatContentTypeError(expected, actual, context string) ValidationError {\n265\t\treturn NewValidationFormatter(\"content_type\").\n266\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n267\t\t\tWithActual(actual).\n268\t\t\tWithContext(context).\n269\t\t\tFormat()\n270\t}\n271\t\n272\t// FormatCustomValidationError creates a validation error with full customization.\n273\t//\n274\t// This function allows complete control over all validation error fields, including\n275\t// custom suggestions. Use this when the predefined convenience functions don't meet\n276\t// your specific requirements.\n277\t//\n278\t// Parameters:\n279\t//   - validationType: The category of validation\n280\t//   - expected: The expected value\n281\t//   - actual: The actual value received\n282\t//   - options: Optional configuration functions to customize the error\n283\t//\n284\t// Example usage:\n285\t//\n286\t//\terr := validate.FormatCustomValidationError(\n287\t//\t    \"custom_field\",\n288\t//\t    \"required_value\",\n289\t//\t    \"actual_value\",\n290\t//\t    validate.WithContext(\"custom validation\"),\n291\t//\t    validate.WithResponseSnippet(`{\"field\": \"actual_value\"}`),\n292\t//\t    validate.WithSuggestions(\"Check field value\", \"Verify configuration\"),\n293\t//\t)\n294\tfunc FormatCustomValidationError(\n295\t\tvalidationType string,\n296\t\texpected, actual interface{},\n297\t\toptions ...FormatOption,\n298\t) ValidationError {\n299\t\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n300\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n301\t\t\topt(config)\n302\t\t}\n303\t\n304\t\tformatter := NewValidationFormatter(validationType).\n305\t\t\tWithExpected(expected).\n306\t\t\tWithActual(actual)\n307\t\n308\t\tif config.Context != \"\" {\n309\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithContext(config.Context)\n310\t\t}\n311\t\tif config.ResponseSnippet != \"\" {\n312\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithResponseSnippet(config.ResponseSnippet)\n313\t\t}\n314\t\tif config.FieldName != \"\" {\n315\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n316\t\t}\n317\t\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n318\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n319\t\t}\n320\t\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n321\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n322\t\t}\n323\t\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n324\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n325\t\t}\n326\t\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n327\t\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n328\t\t}\n329\t\n330\t\treturn formatter.Format()\n331\t}\n332\t\n333\t// =============================================================================\n334\t// FORMAT OPTIONS FOR CUSTOM VALIDATION\n335\t// =============================================================================\n336\t\n337\t// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\n338\ttype FormatConfig struct {\n339\t\tContext            string\n340\t\tResponseSnippet    string\n341\t\tFieldName          string\n342\t\tPatternDetails     string\n343\t\tRangeInfo          string\n344\t\tValidationDetails  []string\n345\t\tSuggestions        []string\n346\t}\n347\t\n348\t// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\n349\ttype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n350\t\n351\t// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\n352\tfunc WithContext(context string) FormatOption {\n353\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n354\t\t\tc.Context = context\n355\t\t}\n356\t}\n357\t\n358\t// WithResponseSnippet creates a FormatOption that sets the response snippet.\n359\tfunc WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n360\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n361\t\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n362\t\t}\n363\t}\n364\t\n365\t// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\n366\tfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n367\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n368\t\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n369\t\t}\n370\t}\n371\t\n372\t// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\n373\tfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n374\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n375\t\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n376\t\t}\n377\t}\n378\t\n379\t// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\n380\tfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n381\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n382\t\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n383\t\t}\n384\t}\n385\t\n386\t// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\n387\tfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n388\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n389\t\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n390\t\t}\n391\t}\n392\t\n393\t// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\n394\tfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n395\t\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n396\t\t\tc.Suggestions = append(c.Suggestions, suggestions...)\n397\t\t}\n398\t}\n399\t\n400\t// =============================================================================\n401\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n402\t// =============================================================================\n403\t\n404\t// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\n405\tfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n406\t\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n407\t\tif err != nil {\n408\t\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n409\t\t}\n410\t\n411\t\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n412\t\tif err != nil {\n413\t\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n414\t\t\tdesc = \"\"\n415\t\t}\n416\t\n417\t\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n418\t}\n419\t\n420\t// =============================================================================\n421\t// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n422\t// =============================================================================\n423\t\n424\t// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n425\t// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\n426\ttype errorTypeTracker struct {\n427\t\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n428\t\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n429\t}\n430\t\n431\t// global tracker instance\n432\tvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n433\t\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\t// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n437\t// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n438\t// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n439\t//\n440\t// Parameters:\n441\t//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n442\t//\n443\t// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\n444\tfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n445\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n446\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n447\t\n448\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n449\t\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n450\t}\n451\t\n452\t// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n453\t// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n454\t// configuration issues.\n455\t//\n456\t// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\n457\tfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n458\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n459\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n460\t\n461\t\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n462\t\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n463\t\t\tresult[k] = v\n464\t\t}\n465\t\treturn result\n466\t}\n467\t\n468\t// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n469\t// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\n470\tfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n471\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n472\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n473\t\n474\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n475\t}\n476\t\n477\t// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n478\t// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\n479\tfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n480\t\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n481\t\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n482\t\n483\t\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n484\t}\n485\t\n486\t// =============================================================================\n487\t// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n488\t// =============================================================================\n489\t\n490\t// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n491\t// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n492\t// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n493\t// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n494\t//\n495\t// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n496\t// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\n497\t// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n498\t//\n499\t// Error Type Validation:\n500\t//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n501\t//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n502\t//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n503\t//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n504\t//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n505\t//\n506\t// Parameters:\n507\t//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n508\t//   - message: The error message content\n509\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n510\t//\n511\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n512\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n513\t//\n514\t// Example usage:\n515\t//\n516\t//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n517\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n518\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n519\t//\n520\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n521\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n522\t//\n523\t//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n524\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n525\t//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n526\t//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n527\t//\n528\t//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n529\t//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n530\t//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n531\t//\n532\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n533\t//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n534\t//\n535\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n536\t//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\n537\tfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n538\t\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n539\t\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n540\t\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n541\t\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n542\t\n543\t\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n544\t\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the original wasn't \"unknown\",\n545\t\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n546\t\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && errorType != \"unknown\" {\n547\t\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n548\t\t}\n549\t\n550\t\t// Extract field name from variadic args\n551\t\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n552\t\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n553\t\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n554\t\t}\n555\t\n556\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n557\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n558\t\n559\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n560\t\tif message == \"\" {\n561\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n562\t\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n563\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n564\t\t\t} else {\n565\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n566\t\t\t}\n567\t\t}\n568\t\n569\t\t// Handle empty errorType - use fallback\n570\t\tif errorType == \"\" {\n571\t\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n572\t\t}\n573\t\n574\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the original error type string\n575\t\t// This maintains backward compatibility - we always use the original string\n576\t\t// even if it wasn't a recognized ErrorType enum value\n577\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\t// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n581\t// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n582\t// instead of string-based error types.\n583\t//\n584\t// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n585\t// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n586\t//\n587\t// Parameters:\n588\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n589\t//   - message: The error message content\n590\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n591\t//\n592\t// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n593\t// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n594\t//\n595\t// Example usage:\n596\t//\n597\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n598\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n599\t//\n600\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n601\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n602\t//\n603\t//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n604\t//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\n605\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n606\t\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n607\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n608\t\n609\t\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n610\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n611\t\n612\t\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n613\t\tif message == \"\" {\n614\t\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n615\t\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n616\t\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n617\t\t\t} else {\n618\t\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n619\t\t\t}\n620\t\t}\n621\t\n622\t\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n623\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n624\t}\n625\t\n626\t// =============================================================================\n627\t// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n628\t// =============================================================================\n629\t\n630\t// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\n631\ttype QuoteStyle string\n632\t\n633\tconst (\n634\t\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n635\t\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n636\t\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n637\t\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n638\t\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n639\t\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n640\t\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n641\t\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n642\t)\n643\t\n644\t// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\n645\ttype FieldRefConfig struct {\n646\t\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n647\t\tprefix     string\n648\t}\n649\t\n650\t// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\n651\ttype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n652\t\n653\t// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n654\t// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n655\t//\n656\t// Example usage:\n657\t//\n658\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n659\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n660\t//\n661\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n662\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n663\tfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n664\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n665\t\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n666\t\t}\n667\t}\n668\t\n669\t// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n670\t// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n671\t// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n672\t//\n673\t// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n674\t//\n675\t// Example usage:\n676\t//\n677\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n678\t//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n679\t//\n680\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n681\t//\t// Returns: request.email\n682\t//\n683\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n684\t//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\n685\tfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n686\t\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n687\t\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n688\t\t}\n689\t}\n690\t\n691\t// =============================================================================\n692\t// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n693\t// =============================================================================\n694\t\n695\t// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n696\t// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n697\t// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n698\t//\n699\t// Parameters:\n700\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n701\t//\n702\t// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n703\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n704\t//\n705\t// Example usage:\n706\t//\n707\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n708\t//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n709\t//\n710\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n711\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n712\t//\n713\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n714\t//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n715\t//\n716\t//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n717\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n718\tfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n719\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n720\t\n721\t\t// Handle empty path\n722\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n723\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n724\t\t}\n725\t\n726\t\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n727\t\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n728\t}\n729\t\n730\t// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n731\t// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\n732\tfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n733\t\tif path == \"\" {\n734\t\t\treturn \"\"\n735\t\t}\n736\t\n737\t\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n738\t\tvar result []string\n739\t\n740\t\tfor _, part := range parts {\n741\t\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n742\t\t\tif part == \"\" {\n743\t\t\t\tcontinue\n744\t\t\t}\n745\t\n746\t\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n747\t\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n748\t\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n749\t\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n750\t\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n751\t\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n752\t\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n753\t\t\t\t} else {\n754\t\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n755\t\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n756\t\t\t\t}\n757\t\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n758\t\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n759\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n760\t\t\t} else {\n761\t\t\t\t// Regular field name\n762\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n763\t\t\t}\n764\t\t}\n765\t\n766\t\tif len(result) == 0 {\n767\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n768\t\t}\n769\t\n770\t\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n771\t}\n772\t\n773\t// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\n774\tfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n775\t\tif s == \"\" {\n776\t\t\treturn false\n777\t\t}\n778\t\n779\t\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n780\t\tif s[0] == '-' {\n781\t\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n782\t\t\t\treturn false\n783\t\t\t}\n784\t\t\ts = s[1:]\n785\t\t}\n786\t\n787\t\t// Check if all characters are digits\n788\t\tfor _, r := range s {\n789\t\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n790\t\t\t\treturn false\n791\t\t\t}\n792\t\t}\n793\t\n794\t\treturn true\n795\t}\n796\t\n797\t// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\n798\tfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n799\t\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n800\t}\n801\t\n802\t// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n803\t// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n804\t// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n805\t// for field names through variadic options.\n806\t//\n807\t// Parameters:\n808\t//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n809\t//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n810\t//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n811\t//\n812\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n813\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n814\t//\n815\t// Example usage:\n816\t//\n817\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n818\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n819\t//\n820\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n821\t//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n822\t//\n823\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n824\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n825\t//\n826\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n827\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n828\t//\n829\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n830\t//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n831\t//\n832\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n833\t//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n834\t//\n835\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n836\t//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n837\t//\n838\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n839\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n840\t//\n841\t//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n842\t//\t// Returns: \"request\"\n843\tfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n844\t\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n845\t\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n846\t\n847\t\t// Apply options\n848\t\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n849\t\tfor _, opt := range options {\n850\t\t\topt(config)\n851\t\t}\n852\t\n853\t\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n854\t\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n855\t\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n856\t\t}\n857\t\n858\t\t// Handle empty field path\n859\t\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n860\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n861\t\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n862\t\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n863\t\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n864\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n865\t\t\t\t}\n866\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n867\t\t\t}\n868\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n869\t\t}\n870\t\n871\t\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n872\t\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n873\t\n874\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n875\t\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n876\t\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n877\t\t\t\treturn prefix\n878\t\t\t}\n879\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n880\t\t}\n881\t\n882\t\t// Apply quote style if specified\n883\t\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n884\t\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n885\t\t}\n886\t\n887\t\t// Add prefix if provided\n888\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n889\t\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n890\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n891\t\n892\t\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n893\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n894\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n895\t\t\t}\n896\t\n897\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n898\t\t}\n899\t\n900\t\treturn normalizedPath\n901\t}\n902\t\n903\t// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n904\t// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\n905\tfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n906\t\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n907\t\t\treturn path\n908\t\t}\n909\t\n910\t\tvar result strings.Builder\n911\t\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n912\t\tinBrackets := false\n913\t\n914\t\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n915\t\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n916\t\n917\t\t\tswitch {\n918\t\t\tcase r == '.':\n919\t\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n920\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 r == '[':\n929\t\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n930\t\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n931\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n932\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n933\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n934\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n935\t\t\t\t}\n936\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n937\t\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n938\t\n939\t\t\tcase r == ']':\n940\t\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n941\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n942\t\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n943\t\n944\t\t\tdefault:\n945\t\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n946\t\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n947\t\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n948\t\t\t\t} else {\n949\t\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n950\t\t\t\t}\n951\t\t\t}\n952\t\t}\n953\t\n954\t\t// Don't forget the last field name\n955\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 FormatValidationErrorWithDetails functionality with a more ergonomic interface.\ntype ValidationFormatter struct {\n\tvalidationType string\n\texpected       interface{}\n\tactual \"error_message\", \"content_type\")\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tformatter := validate.NewValidationFormatter(\"status_code\").\n//\t    WithExpected(200).\n//\t    WithActual(404).\n//\t actual\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithContext sets additional context about the validation operation.\n// Context can include information like the endpoint being tested, operation type, etc.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithContext(context string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.context = context\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithResponseSnippet sets a truncated excerpt from the response for debugging.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.responseSnippet = snippet\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithFieldName sets the specific field name WithValidationDetails(details ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.validationDetails = append(vf.validationDetails, details...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// WithSuggestions sets custom suggestions for guidance.\nfunc (vf *ValidationFormatter) WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) *ValidationFormatter {\n\tvf.customSuggestions = append(vf.customSuggestions, suggestions...)\n\treturn vf\n}\n\n// {\n\tsuggestions := vf.customSuggestions\n\tif len(suggestions) == 0 {\n\t\t// Use auto-generated suggestions if no custom ones provided\n\t\tsuggestions = generateSuggestions(vf.validationType, vf.expected, vf.actual)\n\t}\n\n\t// Construct ValidationError directly to support custom suggestions\n\treturn ValidationError{\n\t\tErrorType:         vf.validationType,\n\t\tExpected: FormatCustomValidationError(\n\tvalidationType string,\n\texpected, actual interface{},\n\toptions ...FormatOption,\n) ValidationError {\n\tconfig := &FormatConfig{}\n\tfor _, opt := range options {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithFieldName(config.FieldName)\n\t}\n\tif config.PatternDetails != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithPatternDetails(config.PatternDetails)\n\t}\n\tif config.RangeInfo != \"\" {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithRangeInfo(config.RangeInfo)\n\t}\n\tif len(config.ValidationDetails) > 0 {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithValidationDetails(config.ValidationDetails...)\n\t}\n\tif len(config.Suggestions) > 0 {\n\t\tformatter = formatter.WithSuggestions(config.Suggestions...)\n\t}\n\n\treturn =============================================================================\n\n// FormatConfig holds configuration options for validation error formatting.\ntype FormatConfig struct {\n\tContext            string\n\tResponseSnippet    string\n\tFieldName          string\n\tPatternDetails     string\n\tRangeInfo          string\n\tValidationDetails  []string\n\tSuggestions        []string\n}\n\n// FormatOption is a function that configures a FormatConfig.\ntype FormatOption func(*FormatConfig)\n\n// WithContext creates a FormatOption that sets the validation context.\nfunc WithContext(context WithResponseSnippet(snippet string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.ResponseSnippet = snippet\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithFieldName creates a FormatOption that sets the field name.\nfunc WithFieldName(fieldName string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.FieldName = fieldName\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPatternDetails creates a FormatOption that sets pattern details.\nfunc WithPatternDetails(details string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.PatternDetails = details\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithRangeInfo creates a FormatOption that sets range information.\nfunc WithRangeInfo(info string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.RangeInfo = info\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithValidationDetails creates a FormatOption that adds validation details.\nfunc WithValidationDetails(details ...string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.ValidationDetails = append(c.ValidationDetails, details...)\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithSuggestions creates a FormatOption that sets custom suggestions.\nfunc WithSuggestions(suggestions ...string) FormatOption {\n\treturn func(c *FormatConfig) {\n\t\tc.Suggestions = =============================================================================\n\n// getRangeInfo extracts range information from a pattern string.\nfunc getRangeInfo(pattern string) (min, max int, desc string, err error) {\n\tmin, max, err = ParseStatusCodeRange(pattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn 0, 0, \"\", err\n\t}\n\n\tdesc, err = GetStatusCodeRangeDescription(pattern)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\t// Return parsed range even if description fails\n\t\tdesc = \"\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn min, max, desc, nil\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE VALIDATION TRACKING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// errorTypeTracker tracks invalid error types encountered during validation.\n// This helps identify typos, deprecated error types, or configuration issues.\ntype errorTypeTracker struct {\n\tmu       sync.RWMutex\n\tinvalidTypes map[string]int // error type -> count\n}\n\n// global tracker instance\nvar globalErrorTypeTracker = &errorTypeTracker{\n\tinvalidTypes: make(map[string]int),\n}\n\n// TrackInvalidErrorType records an invalid error type for later analysis.\n// This is called automatically by FormatError when an error type doesn't\n// match any known ErrorType enum value.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The invalid error type string that was encountered\n//\n// Returns the current count for this error type (how many times it's been seen).\nfunc TrackInvalidErrorType(errorType string) int {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]++\n\treturn globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes[errorType]\n}\n\n// GetInvalidErrorTypes returns a snapshot of all invalid error types encountered\n// and their occurrence counts. This is useful for debugging and identifying\n// configuration issues.\n//\n// Returns a copy of the invalid types map to avoid concurrent modification issues.\nfunc GetInvalidErrorTypes() map[string]int {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\tresult := make(map[string]int, len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes))\n\tfor k, v := range globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes {\n\t\tresult[k] = v\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking clears all tracked invalid error types.\n// This is primarily useful for testing to ensure a clean state between tests.\nfunc ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking() {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Lock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.Unlock()\n\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes = make(map[string]int)\n}\n\n// InvalidErrorTypeCount returns the total number of invalid error types tracked\n// (not the total occurrences, but the number of unique invalid types).\nfunc InvalidErrorTypeCount() int {\n\tglobalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RLock()\n\tdefer globalErrorTypeTracker.mu.RUnlock()\n\n\treturn len(globalErrorTypeTracker.invalidTypes)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// BASIC ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatError creates a basic formatted error message string.\n// This is a simplified formatting function for basic error message display.\n// Unlike the ValidationFormatter which returns ValidationError structs,\n// this function returns a simple formatted string for quick error display.\n//\n// This function supports both string-based error types (for backward compatibility)\n// and validates them against the ErrorType enum for type-safe error classification.\n// The ErrorType enum provides consistent error classification across all validation contexts.\n//\n// Error Type Validation:\n//   - String error types are validated against the ErrorType enum (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"range\")\n//   - If the error type is not a recognized ErrorType, it is tracked for debugging purposes\n//   - Invalid error types do NOT cause errors - the original string is still used for backward compatibility\n//   - To check what invalid error types have been encountered, use GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n//   - To reset tracking between tests, use ResetInvalidErrorTypeTracking()\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type/category of error (e.g., \"required\", \"format\", \"status_code\")\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\t// Valid error types (recognized by ErrorType enum)\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"required\", \"This field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: This field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n//\n//\t// Custom/unknown error types (still work, but are tracked)\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"custom_validation\", \"Custom check failed\", \"field\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[custom_validation] field: Custom check failed\"\n//\t// \"custom_validation\" is now tracked as an invalid error type\n//\n//\t// Check what invalid types have been encountered\n//\tinvalidTypes := validate.GetInvalidErrorTypes()\n//\t// Returns: map[string]int{\"custom_validation\": 1, ...}\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"\", \"Something went wrong\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[error] Something went wrong\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatError(\"validation\", \"\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[validation] email: email validation failed\"\nfunc FormatError(errorType string, message string, fieldName ...string) string {\n\t// Validate string errorType against ErrorType enum\n\t// This ensures that string-based error types are checked for validity\n\t// while maintaining backward compatibility with any string value\n\tvalidatedType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorType)\n\n\t// Track invalid error types for debugging\n\t// If ErrorTypeFromString returns ErrTypeUnknown but the original wasn't \"unknown\",\n\t// it means we encountered an unrecognized error type string\n\tif validatedType == ErrTypeUnknown && errorType != \"\" && errorType != \"unknown\" {\n\t\tTrackInvalidErrorType(errorType)\n\t}\n\n\t// Extract field name from variadic args\n\tfieldNameStr := \"\"\n\tif len(fieldName) > 0 {\n\t\tfieldNameStr = fieldName[0]\n\t}\n\n\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\n\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n\tif message == \"\" {\n\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n\t\tif fieldNameStr != \"\" {\n\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldNameStr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle empty errorType - use fallback\n\tif errorType == \"\" {\n\t\terrorType = \"error\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Use FormatErrorMessage with the original error type string\n\t// This maintains backward compatibility - we always use the original string\n\t// even if it wasn't a recognized ErrorType enum value\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType, message, fieldNameStr)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithType creates a basic formatted error message string using ErrorType enum.\n// This function provides type-safe error classification using the ErrorType enum\n// instead of string-based error types.\n//\n// This is the preferred method for error formatting when you have an ErrorType enum\n// value, as it ensures consistent error classification and prevents typos.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred (can be empty string)\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with consistent structure.\n// Handles empty/nil inputs gracefully by returning a default format.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n//\n//\tmsg := validate.FormatErrorWithType(validate.ErrTypeRange, \"Value out of range\", \"age\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[range] age: Value out of range\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\t// Convert ErrorType enum to string for formatting\n\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n\n\t// Trim whitespace from message before checking\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\n\t// Handle empty message - use fallback\n\tif message == \"\" {\n\t\t// Check if field name was provided\n\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\t\tmessage = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s validation failed\", fieldName)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tmessage = \"(no message provided)\"\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Use FormatErrorMessage for consistent formatting\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE QUOTE STYLES\n// =============================================================================\n\n// QuoteStyle defines the quoting style for field names in field references.\ntype QuoteStyle string\n\nconst (\n\t// NoQuote applies no quotes to field names\n\tNoQuote QuoteStyle = \"\"\n\t// SingleQuote applies single quotes around field names\n\tSingleQuote QuoteStyle = \"'\"\n\t// DoubleQuote applies double quotes around field names\n\tDoubleQuote QuoteStyle = \"\\\"\"\n\t// Backtick applies backtick quotes around field names\n\tBacktick QuoteStyle = \"`\"\n)\n\n// FieldRefConfig holds configuration options for field reference formatting.\ntype FieldRefConfig struct {\n\tquoteStyle QuoteStyle\n\tprefix     string\n}\n\n// FieldRefOption is a function that configures a FieldRefConfig.\ntype FieldRefOption func(*FieldRefConfig)\n\n// WithQuoteStyle creates a FieldRefOption that sets the quote style for field names.\n// The quote style is applied to each field name component (not to array indices or prefixes).\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\nfunc WithQuoteStyle(style QuoteStyle) FieldRefOption {\n\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n\t\tc.quoteStyle = style\n\t}\n}\n\n// WithPrefix creates a FieldRefOption that sets the prefix for field references.\n// The prefix is added before the field path (e.g., \"response\", \"request\", \"data\").\n// The prefix is not affected by quote styles and is added as-is.\n//\n// If both the prefix parameter and WithPrefix option are provided, the option takes precedence.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"response\"))\n//\t// Returns: response.user.email\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"request\"))\n//\t// Returns: request.email\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithPrefix(\"data\"), WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n//\t// Returns: data.\"users\"[0].\"email\"\nfunc WithPrefix(prefix string) FieldRefOption {\n\treturn func(c *FieldRefConfig) {\n\t\tc.prefix = prefix\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD PATH FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldPath formats a field path for consistent display in error messages.\n// This function handles nested field paths, array indices, and provides graceful\n// handling of edge cases like empty paths or invalid formats.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string ready for use in error messages.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"user.email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"user.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"users.0.email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"data.items[5]\")\n//\t// Returns: \"data.items[5]\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldPath(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldPath(fieldPath string) string {\n\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n\n\t// Handle empty path\n\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Parse and normalize the field path\n\treturn normalizeFieldPath(fieldPath)\n}\n\n// normalizeFieldPath processes a field path and normalizes array notation.\n// It converts \"users.0.email\" to \"users[0].email\" format for consistency.\nfunc normalizeFieldPath(path string) string {\n\tif path == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tparts := strings.Split(path, \".\")\n\tvar result []string\n\n\tfor _, part := range parts {\n\t\tpart = strings.TrimSpace(part)\n\t\tif part == \"\" {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Check if this part is a numeric index (array accessor)\n\t\tif isNumericIndex(part) {\n\t\t\t// Convert \".0\" to \"[0]\" format\n\t\t\tif len(result) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t// Append to previous part as array index\n\t\t\t\tlastIdx := len(result) - 1\n\t\t\t\tresult[lastIdx] = fmt.Sprintf(\"%s[%s]\", result[lastIdx], part)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// Standalone index at start - keep as is\n\t\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else if containsArrayNotation(part) {\n\t\t\t// Already has array notation like \"items[5]\" - keep as is\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Regular field name\n\t\t\tresult = append(result, part)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(result) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(result, \".\")\n}\n\n// isNumericIndex checks if a string represents a numeric array index.\nfunc isNumericIndex(s string) bool {\n\tif s == \"\" {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle negative numbers (though unusual for indices)\n\tif s[0] == '-' {\n\t\tif len(s) == 1 {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t\ts = s[1:]\n\t}\n\n\t// Check if all characters are digits\n\tfor _, r := range s {\n\t\tif r < '0' || r > '9' {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn true\n}\n\n// containsArrayNotation checks if a string already contains array notation like \"[5]\".\nfunc containsArrayNotation(s string) bool {\n\treturn strings.Contains(s, \"[\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n// for field names through variadic options.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" (or \"prefix.(unknown field)\" if prefix provided).\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"user.email\", \"response\", WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\n//\t// Returns: response.\"user\".\"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"users.0.email\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(SingleQuote))\n//\t// Returns: 'users'[0].'email'\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"data.items\", \"\", WithQuoteStyle(Backtick))\n//\t// Returns: `data`.`items`\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReference(\"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request\"\nfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n\n\t// Apply options\n\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n\tfor _, opt := range options {\n\t\topt(config)\n\t}\n\n\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle empty field path\n\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn prefix\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn prefix\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply quote style if specified\n\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n\t}\n\n\t// Add prefix if provided\n\tif prefix != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n\n\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedPath\n}\n\n// applyQuoteStyle applies quotes to field names in a normalized path.\n// Array indices are NOT quoted (e.g., \"users[0].email\" becomes \"'users'[0].'email'\").\nfunc applyQuoteStyle(path string, style QuoteStyle) string {\n\tif path == \"(unknown field)\" || path == \"\" {\n\t\treturn path\n\t}\n\n\tvar result strings.Builder\n\tvar fieldName strings.Builder\n\tinBrackets := false\n\n\tfor i := 0; i < len(path); i++ {\n\t\tr := rune(path[i])\n\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase r == '.':\n\t\t\t// End of field name, add quoted version\n\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('.')\n\n\t\tcase r == '[':\n\t\t\t// Start of array index, flush field name first\n\t\t\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t\t\t\tfieldName.Reset()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tresult.WriteRune('[')\n\t\t\tinBrackets = true\n\n\t\tcase r == ']':\n\t\t\t// End of array index, don't quote\n\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(']')\n\t\t\tinBrackets = false\n\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t// Only accumulate field name characters when NOT inside brackets\n\t\t\tif !inBrackets {\n\t\t\t\tfieldName.WriteRune(r)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tresult.WriteRune(r)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Don't forget the last field name\n\tif fieldName.Len() > 0 {\n\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t\tresult.WriteString(fieldName.String())\n\t\tresult.WriteString(string(style))\n\t}\n\n\treturn result.String()\n}\n\n","numLines":964,"startLine":1,"totalLines":964}}}

Main Implementation\n\n**File:** `/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/format_helper.go`\n\nThe `FormatFieldReference` function is implemented at **lines 802-901** with comprehensive documentation:\n\n```go\n// FormatFieldReference creates a standardized field reference string with an optional prefix.\n// This function formats field paths for consistent display in error messages, normalizing\n// array notation and handling edge cases gracefully. It supports optional quote styles\n// for field names through variadic options.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldPath: The field path to format (e.g., \"user.email\", \"users.0.email\", \"data.items[5]\")\n//   - prefix: Optional field prefix to add (e.g., \"request\", \"response\", \"\" for no prefix)\n//   - options: Optional functions to configure formatting (e.g., WithQuoteStyle(DoubleQuote))\nfunc FormatFieldReference(fieldPath string, prefix string, options ...FieldRefOption) string {\n\tfieldPath = strings.TrimSpace(fieldPath)\n\tprefix = strings.TrimSpace(prefix)\n\n\t// Apply options\n\tconfig := &FieldRefConfig{quoteStyle: NoQuote, prefix: \"\"}\n\tfor _, opt := range options {\n\t\topt(config)\n\t}\n\n\t// If prefix was set via option, use it (overrides the direct parameter)\n\tif config.prefix != \"\" {\n\t\tprefix = config.prefix\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle empty field path\n\tif fieldPath == \"\" {\n\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize prefix even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n\t\t\tif normalizedPrefix != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedPrefix\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn prefix\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field path to handle array indices\n\tnormalizedPath := FormatFieldPath(fieldPath)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedPath == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif prefix != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn prefix\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Apply quote style if specified\n\tif config.quoteStyle != NoQuote {\n\t\tnormalizedPath = applyQuoteStyle(normalizedPath, config.quoteStyle)\n\t}\n\n\t// Add prefix if provided\n\tif prefix != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize prefix as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedPrefix := FormatFieldPath(prefix)\n\n\t\t// If prefix normalizes to unknown field, just return the field path\n\t\tif normalizedPrefix == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedPath\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedPrefix, normalizedPath)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedPath\n}\n```\n\n## 2.
